2605.19141.title: GRASP: Deterministic argument ranking in interaction graphs
2605.19141.paper_summary: Large language models are increasingly deployed as automated judges to evaluate the strength of arguments. As this role expands, their legitimacy depends on consistency, transparency, and the ability to separate argumentative structure from rhetorical appeal. However, we show that holistic judging - a common LLM-as-a-Judge practice where a model provides a global verdict on a debate - suffers from substantial inter-model disagreement. We argue that this instability arises from collapsing a debate's complex interaction structure into a single opaque score. To address this, we propose GRASP (Gradual Ranking with Attacks and Support Propagation), a deterministic framework that aggregates stable local interaction judgments into a global ranking via a convergent attack--defense propagation operator. We show that local interaction judgments are more reproducible than holistic rankings in LLM-as-a-Judge evaluations, allowing GRASP to produce more consistent global rankings. We further show that GRASP scores do not correlate with human "convincingness" labels, highlighting a vital sociotechnical distinction: GRASP does not measure persuasion, factuality, or rhetorical appeal, but structural sufficiency - a defense-aware notion of argument robustness over the explicit interaction graph. Overall, GRASP offers a transparent and auditable alternative to holistic LLM judging.
2605.19141.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地被用作自動評判者，以評估論點的強度。隨著這一角色的擴展，它們的合法性依賴於一致性、透明度以及將論證結構與修辭吸引力分開的能力。 然而，我們顯示出整體評判——一種常見的 LLM 作為評判者的做法，其中模型對辯論提供全球判決——存在顯著的模型間不一致。我們認為，這種不穩定性源於將辯論的複雜互動結構簡化為單一不透明的分數。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 GRASP（逐步排名與攻擊和支持傳播），這是一個確定性的框架，通過收斂的攻擊-防禦傳播運算符將穩定的局部互動判斷聚合成全球排名。我們顯示，在 LLM 作為評判者的評估中，局部互動判斷比整體排名更具可重現性，使 GRASP 能夠產生更一致的全球排名。我們進一步顯示，GRASP 分數與人類的「說服力」標籤不相關，突顯出一個重要的社會技術區別：GRASP 不測量說服力、事實性或修辭吸引力，而是結構充分性——一種對明確互動圖的防禦意識的論證穩健性概念。總的來說，GRASP 提供了一種透明且可審計的替代方案，以取代整體 LLM 評判。
2605.19141.author: Diganta Misra et.al.
2605.19141.authors: Diganta Misra, Antonio Orvieto, Rediet Abebe, Volkan Cevher
